examples.title: Digital vs Analog Scale
examples.content: Digital scale reads 65.247 kg. Analog scale reads 65.2 kg. Digital is MORE PRECISE (3 decimal places vs 1). But both could be wrong by 2 kg (inaccurate).

examples.title: Stopwatch Precision
examples.content: Cheap stopwatch: 10.5 seconds (one decimal). Precise stopwatch: 10.543 seconds (three decimals). More decimal places = more precision.

examples.title: Precise but Wrong
examples.content: A scale always reads 70 kg when you weigh yourself (your true weight is 75 kg). Super consistent/precise (same answer every time) but wrong/inaccurate (5 kg off).
